description: Replace the existing Angular frontend with a full recipe CRUD application. Includes authentication (login/signup via Cognito), recipe list view, recipe detail view, and recipe create/edit form. Uses Angular standalone components with reactive forms.
steps: Install frontend dependencies: add @aws-amplify/auth (for Cognito integration) or use amazon-cognito-identity-js directly. Actually, use the lightweight approach with amazon-cognito-identity-js added to package.json dependencies. Also add @angular/material for UI components (optional but recommended for forms), or keep it simple with plain HTML/SCSS.
Create apps/web/src/environments/environment.ts and environment.prod.ts with placeholder config: apiUrl, userPoolId, userPoolClientId, region. These will be filled after deployment.

Create an auth service at apps/web/src/app/auth/auth.service.ts that wraps amazon-cognito-identity-js: signUp(email, password), confirmSignUp(email, code), signIn(email, password), signOut(), getIdToken(): Observable<string|null>, isAuthenticated(): Observable<boolean>, getCurrentUser(). Store tokens in memory/localStorage.

Create auth components: apps/web/src/app/auth/login.component.ts (email + password form with sign-in and link to register), apps/web/src/app/auth/register.component.ts (email + password form with sign-up), apps/web/src/app/auth/confirm.component.ts (verification code input after registration). All standalone components with OnPush change detection.

Create a recipe service at apps/web/src/app/recipes/recipe.service.ts that calls the API: getRecipes(), getRecipe(id), createRecipe(input), updateRecipe(id, input), deleteRecipe(id). Use HttpClient with an auth interceptor that adds the Cognito ID token as Authorization header.
Create an HTTP interceptor at apps/web/src/app/auth/auth.interceptor.ts that injects the Authorization: Bearer <idToken> header on API requests.

Create recipe list component at apps/web/src/app/recipes/recipe-list.component.ts with template showing a grid/list of recipe cards (title, description preview, prep time, cook time, categories). Include a 'New Recipe' button.

Create recipe detail component at apps/web/src/app/recipes/recipe-detail.component.ts showing full recipe: title, description, time info, servings, ingredients list grouped by group, numbered instructions, categories/tags.

Create recipe form component at apps/web/src/app/recipes/recipe-form.component.ts with reactive form for create/edit: title, description, servings, prepTimeMinutes, cookTimeMinutes, dynamic ingredient array (add/remove), dynamic instruction array (add/remove/reorder), categories input (comma-separated or chips), tags input. Used for both create and edit routes.

Update apps/web/src/app/app.routes.ts with routes: '' -> redirect to /recipes, 'login' -> LoginComponent, 'register' -> RegisterComponent, 'confirm' -> ConfirmComponent, 'recipes' -> RecipeListComponent (guarded), 'recipes/new' -> RecipeFormComponent (guarded), 'recipes/:id' -> RecipeDetailComponent (guarded), 'recipes/:id/edit' -> RecipeFormComponent (guarded).
Create an auth guard at apps/web/src/app/auth/auth.guard.ts that redirects to /login if not authenticated.
Update apps/web/src/app/app.component.ts and app.component.html to have a simple layout shell: header with app title and login/logout button, router-outlet for content.
Update apps/web/src/app/app.config.ts to include provideHttpClient(withInterceptors([authInterceptor])) and provideRouter(appRoutes).
Update apps/web/src/styles.scss with basic global styles (clean, modern look - simple CSS, no external framework needed).
acceptance_criteria: pnpm nx build web succeeds without errors
App has login, register, and confirm routes for authentication
App has recipe list, detail, create, and edit routes
Auth interceptor adds Authorization header to API calls
Recipe form supports dynamic ingredients and instructions arrays
All components use standalone: true and ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush
verification: Run: cd /projects/sandbox/recipe-manager && pnpm nx build web
Run: cd /projects/sandbox/recipe-manager && pnpm exec tsc --project apps/web/tsconfig.app.json --noEmit
findings: Build succeeds with warnings about CommonJS dependencies from amazon-cognito-identity-js (buffer, @aws-crypto/sha256-js, isomorphic-unfetch). These are benign optimization warnings and do not affect functionality. Used Angular 21 new @if/@for control flow syntax. All components are standalone with OnPush change detection. The auth interceptor uses functional HttpInterceptorFn pattern with inject(). Auth guard uses functional CanActivateFn pattern.
